creator: c.kworr@gmail.com
text:
Actually I don't think the patch is correct per se. Yet I deployed it on the problematic server and everything magically worked.

The problems with patch is that Postgresql pid file, actually, holds more data then ordinary pid file:

# cat /usr/local/pgsql/data/postmaster.pid
62483
/usr/local/pgsql/data
1459888343
5432
/tmp
localhost
  5432001    196609

So when other commands are removed from `rc.d/postgresql` it fails to 'stop', 'status' and 'restart'.

I really don't know why but the server became stable after this patch. Maybe the cause of the problem was fixed in the postgresql sources or maybe showing postgresql pid file to rc.subr did that - I have no idea.
